Glossary:
# Glossary

Quick definitions for every acronym and term used across all DeepVault notes — Computer Systems, C++, Game Math, Unreal Engine, and Algorithms.

## Computer Systems

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **ALU** | Arithmetic Logic Unit — the circuit inside the CPU that performs addition, subtraction, bitwise operations, and comparisons |
| **ASLR** | Address Space Layout Randomization — randomizes where code, stack, and heap are placed in memory to make buffer overflow attacks harder |
| **Branch Predictor** | A circuit that guesses whether a conditional jump will be taken, keeping the pipeline full |
| **DRAM** | Dynamic Random Access Memory — stores each bit as charge in a capacitor that must be refreshed ~64 ms |
| **ELF** | Executable and Linkable Format — the file format for executables on Linux |
| **Futex** | Fast Userspace muTEX — Linux kernel primitive for efficient locking; fast path in userspace, slow path in kernel |
| **FTL** | Flash Translation Layer — SSD controller that maps logical blocks to physical NAND pages, handles wear leveling |
| **GOT** | Global Offset Table — holds actual addresses of external functions in dynamically-linked executables |
| **IPC** | Instructions Per Clock — how many instructions the CPU completes per cycle; modern CPUs achieve 2-4 IPC |
| **IR** | Intermediate Representation — compiler-internal format between source and machine code (LLVM IR, GCC GIMPLE) |
| **KPTI** | Kernel Page Table Isolation — Meltdown mitigation; kernel memory unmapped from userspace page tables |
| **LAPIC** | Local Advanced Programmable Interrupt Controller — per-core interrupt controller for timer interrupts |
| **LRU** | Least Recently Used — page replacement policy evicting the page accessed furthest in the past |
| **LSB / MSB** | Least/Most Significant Byte — byte at lowest/highest positional weight in a multi-byte number |
| **MESI** | Modified, Exclusive, Shared, Invalid — four-state cache coherence protocol |
| **MLFQ** | Multi-Level Feedback Queue — scheduling with multiple priority queues; threads demoted if CPU-bound |
| **MMU** | Memory Management Unit — hardware translating virtual addresses to physical via page table and TLB |
| **μop** | Micro-operation — complex x86 instructions decoded into simpler internal μops for execution |
| **NAND Flash** | Storage technology behind SSDs; stores bits by trapping charge in floating-gate transistors |
| **NUMA** | Non-Uniform Memory Access — multi-socket systems where CPUs have faster access to local RAM |
| **NX / DEP** | No-eXecute / Data Execution Prevention — marks memory pages non-executable |
| **PE** | Portable Executable — file format for Windows executables (EXE, DLL) |
| **PLT** | Procedure Linkage Table — stubs for dynamically-linked functions; resolved on first call |
| **PTE** | Page Table Entry — stores physical frame number, permissions, and flags |
| **RAID** | Redundant Array of Inexpensive Disks — combining disks for speed, reliability, or both |
| **ROB** | Reorder Buffer — CPU structure tracking in-flight instructions; retires in program order |
| **SMT** | Simultaneous Multi-Threading (Hyper-Threading) — single core exposes two logical cores |
| **SSE / AVX** | Streaming SIMD Extensions / Advanced Vector Extensions — x86 vector operation instruction sets |
| **TLB** | Translation Lookaside Buffer — small fast cache inside MMU for recent virtual→physical translations |
| **VMA** | Virtual Memory Area — kernel data structure describing one contiguous region of virtual address space |
| **vDSO** | Virtual Dynamic Shared Object — small kernel-mapped library for fast syscalls in userspace |

## C++ Language

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **ADL** | Argument-Dependent Lookup — also called Koenig lookup; function name lookup that searches namespaces of its arguments |
| **constexpr** | Keyword marking a function or variable as evaluable at compile time |
| **CRTP** | Curiously Recurring Template Pattern — a class inherits from a template instantiated with itself: `class Derived : public Base<Derived>` |
| **CTAD** | Class Template Argument Deduction (C++17) — compiler deduces template arguments from constructor arguments |
| **Lvalue / Rvalue** | Lvalue has a persistent address; Rvalue is temporary. Moved-from objects use `std::move` |
| **NRVO / RVO** | Named Return Value Optimization / Return Value Optimization — compiler elides copy/move when returning a local object |
| **ODR** | One Definition Rule — each entity must have exactly one definition across all translation units |
| **OOP** | Object-Oriented Programming — encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism |
| **POD** | Plain Old Data — a trivial, standard-layout type with no virtual functions or complex constructors |
| **RAII** | Resource Acquisition Is Initialization — tie resource lifetime to object lifetime via constructor/destructor |
| **RTTI** | Run-Time Type Information — `typeid` and `dynamic_cast` mechanism for polymorphic types |
| **SFINAE** | Substitution Failure Is Not An Error — template metaprogramming technique where invalid substitutions are silently discarded |
| **SSO** | Small String Optimization — `std::string` stores short strings inline without heap allocation (typically 15-23 chars) |
| **STL** | Standard Template Library — containers, algorithms, iterators, and function objects |
| **TCO** | Tail Call Optimization — compiler reuses stack frame when last action is calling another function |
| **UB** | Undefined Behavior — code whose behavior is not defined by the standard; anything can happen |

## Game Math

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **AABB** | Axis-Aligned Bounding Box — a rectangular box whose edges are parallel to the coordinate axes; used for broad-phase collision |
| **BRDF** | Bidirectional Reflectance Distribution Function — describes how light reflects off a surface |
| **CCD** | Continuous Collision Detection — detects collisions between frames by sweeping geometry along its trajectory |
| **Dot Product** | Scalar product of two vectors: a·b = `|a||b|cosθ`. Measures alignment: 1 = parallel, 0 = perpendicular, -1 = opposite |
| **Cross Product** | Vector product: a×b produces a vector perpendicular to both, magnitude = `|a||b|sinθ` |
| **FFT** | Fast Fourier Transform — converts signals from time domain to frequency domain; used in audio and image processing |
| **GJK** | Gilbert-Johnson-Keerthi algorithm — narrow-phase collision detection for convex shapes |
| **IK** | Inverse Kinematics — computing joint angles from an end-effector target position (e.g., foot placement) |
| **LERL** | Linear Interpolation: `L(t) = A + t(B-A)`. Moves between two values at constant speed |
| **MVP** | Model-View-Projection matrix — transforms vertices from local space through world, camera, and clip space |
| **Quaternion** | 4D extension of complex numbers; represents 3D rotations without gimbal lock |
| **SAT** | Separating Axis Theorem — narrow-phase collision for convex polyhedra: shapes overlap iff no separating axis exists |
| **SLERP** | Spherical Linear Interpolation — smooth interpolation between quaternion rotations along a geodesic on the unit sphere |
| **Vector** | A quantity with magnitude and direction; in game math, typically a 3-component (x,y,z) or 4-component (x,y,z,w) tuple |

## Unreal Engine

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **Actor** | Base class for objects that can be placed in a level; has Tick, can replicate |
| **BP** | Blueprint — UE's visual scripting system |
| **CDO** | Class Default Object — template instance holding default property values for a UCLASS |
| **GAS** | Gameplay Ability System — UE's framework for abilities, attributes, and gameplay effects |
| **GC** | Garbage Collection — UE's automatic memory management for UObjects |
| **Pawn** | An Actor that can be possessed by a Controller (player or AI) |
| **RPC** | Remote Procedure Call — function call sent across the network (Server, Client, NetMulticast) |
| **UCLASS** | Macro marking a class for UE reflection; enables GC, serialization, Blueprint exposure |
| **UFUNCTION** | Macro marking a function for Blueprint call, network RPC, or exec command |
| **UHT** | Unreal Header Tool — parses UCLASS/UPROPERTY/UFUNCTION macros, generates reflection code |
| **UObject** | Base class of all UE objects; provides reflection, serialization, garbage collection |
| **UPROPERTY** | Macro marking a member for GC tracking, serialization, replication, or Blueprint access |

## Algorithms & Complexity

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **O(1)** | Constant time — execution time does not depend on input size. Example: array index lookup |
| **O(log n)** | Logarithmic time — each step halves the problem size. Example: binary search |
| **O(n)** | Linear time — execution time scales 1:1 with input. Example: linear scan through array |
| **O(n log n)** | Linearithmic time — typical of efficient sorting. Example: merge sort, quicksort |
| **O(n²)** | Quadratic time — nested loops. Example: brute-force collision checking |
| **O(2^n)** | Exponential time — execution doubles with each additional input. Intractable for game code |
| **Amortized Analysis** | Average cost per operation over a sequence, not worst-case per operation. Example: vector push_back is O(1) amortized |
| **Divide & Conquer** | Algorithm paradigm: split problem, solve sub-problems, combine results. Example: merge sort |
| **Dynamic Programming** | Solving complex problems by breaking into overlapping sub-problems with memoization |

DeepVault Guide:
# DeepVault Guide

Welcome to DeepVault — an interactive learning fortress for mastering C++, Unreal Engine, and the computer systems beneath them.

## How This Works

Every folder on the dashboard contains **Learn** notes and **Quiz** exercises:

| Mode | What It Does |
|---|---|
| **Learn** | The full vault note, rendered exactly as it appears in Obsidian. Read, scroll the right-side table of contents, click wiki links to jump between notes. |
| **Quiz** | 5-10 randomized questions per note — multiple choice and text input. Instant feedback. Scores saved locally on your device. |

### Tracking Your Progress

Progress is based on **quiz scores only** — reading a note does not count. Here's how it works:

| Status | What It Means |
|---|---|
| **NOT STARTED** | You haven't taken the quiz yet |
| **IN PROGRESS** | At least 1 attempt, but best score is below 80% |
| **COMPLETED** | You scored 80% or higher on at least one attempt |

The folder header shows your overall completion percentage. Quiz scores and attempt history are saved locally via localStorage — they never leave your device.

**Wiki links** like [[04 — CPU Architecture]] connect notes to each other. Click any link to jump to that note's Learn page.

**Recent notes** appear on the dashboard below your folders — quick jump back to what you were studying.

---

## Master Study Order

This vault is designed to be read in order. Each note builds on the ones before it. See the [[Learning Path]] for the full reading order — this is just the big-picture map.

### Layer 1 — Computer Systems (start here)

Without understanding the hardware, C++ is just magic words. Start with the physical reality.

Covering: bits and bytes → assembly → CPU → memory hierarchy → virtual memory → storage → DMA → networking → boot process → GPU → interrupts → system software → concurrency. Over 50 notes from transistor to thread pool.

Start with [[01 — Bits, Bytes & Number Systems]] and follow the [[Learning Path — Computer Systems|Computer Systems Learning Path]].

### Layer 2 — C++ Fundamentals

Once you understand the hardware, learn the language that runs on it.

45 notes across 6 phases: Primer (absolute beginner) → Syntax & Types → Memory → OOP & Generics → Modern C++ → Engine Bridge. Every note has exercises.

Start with [[01 — Primer/00 — Primer Overview|C++ Primer]] and follow the [[Learning Path — C++ Fundamentals|C++ Learning Path]].

### Layer 3 — Game Math

The mathematics that make 3D worlds possible.

41 notes across 10 subfolders: Trigonometry → Matrices → Quaternions → Collision Detection → Physics Simulation → Animation → Navigation → Rendering → Procedural Generation → Audio & Networking → Geometric Algebra → UE Bridge. Framework-agnostic (pure C++ structs, not UE-specific).

Start with [[From Trigonometry to 3D Transformations]] and follow the [[Learning Path — Game Math|Game Math Learning Path]].

### Layer 4 — Unreal Engine Core

How UE turns C++ into a game engine.

14 notes: UHT & Macros → Pointer Ecosystem → String Types → Casting → Containers → Templates → Move Semantics.

Start with [[UHT, Macros & Reflection]] and follow the [[Learning Path — Unreal Engine Core|UE Core Learning Path]].

### Layer 5 — UE Networking

Multiplayer from the hardware up.

7 notes: Network Roles → RPCs → RepNotifies → PushModel → Iris Replication → Iris Filtering → GAS Networking.

Start with [[Network Roles]] and follow the [[Learning Path — Unreal Engine Networking|UE Networking Learning Path]].

### Layer 6 — Big O & Algorithms

6 notes: Complexity classes → Amortized analysis → Applied examples → Sorting algorithms → Graph algorithms → Dynamic programming.

Start with [[The Big O Complexity Spectrum]] and follow the [[Learning Path — Algorithms & Complexity|Algorithms Learning Path]].

---

## Reference

- [[Glossary]] — Every acronym defined (MMU, TLB, PTE, MESI, PLT, GOT...)
- [[Learning Path]] — All six category paths
- Use the right-side table of contents to jump between sections in any note
- Check the dashboard for recently viewed notes and quiz scores

---

DeepVault is built for Mayk's vault. All notes are source-verified against UE 5.8 engine code. 

### Moving Between Devices

Your quiz progress lives in your browser's localStorage. To continue studying on another device:

1. Export on your current device — downloads a JSON file
2. Transfer the file (email, AirDrop, cloud)
3. Import on the new device — merges all your scores

Export and Import buttons are at the bottom of the dashboard. No account needed.

---

Double-click index.html to launch. Everything stays on your device.
